title: Parkio Daily for June 20, 2026
teaser: Cinderella Castle refresh is done; BoardWalk gets a new nightclub and restaurant; Wilderness Lodge faces AC outage during heat wave.

rides.name: Star Tours
rides.note: New merch in Tatooine Traders today

breaking.title: Wilderness Lodge AC outage hits during heat advisory
breaking.body: Multiple-day air conditioning failure at Disney's Wilderness Lodge has left deluxe rooms in the 90-degree range amid a Central Florida heat advisory. Restaurant reservations at the resort are currently unavailable.

parkioInsight.text: Guests with Wilderness Lodge reservations this weekend may want to contact Disney about relocation options. The resort tends to run hot even with working AC during summer afternoons.

bignews.title: Cinderella Castle repainting project complete
bignews.body: Magic Kingdom's icon refresh, which began earlier this year with minimal guest impact, is now finished. The castle received a full repaint with enhanced detailing and brighter colors.

parkioInsight.text: Morning light between 8–9am tends to offer the best photo conditions on the newly refreshed castle. Hub crowds are usually thinner before 10am.

bignews.title: BoardWalk nightclub, restaurant, and retail confirmed for late 2026
bignews.body: Disney announced new dining, nightlife, and shopping experiences coming to BoardWalk following recent vacancies. The venues will include a new lounge and restaurant concept inspired by turn-of-the-century coastal boardwalks.

parkioInsight.text: Current BoardWalk dining options tend to fill up by 7pm on weekends. Worth booking reservations early if visiting before the new venues open later this year.

bignews.title: EPCOT Parisian Breakfast Buffet returns for Food & Wine Festival
bignews.body: Chefs de France will once again offer the all-you-can-eat French breakfast with bottomless mimosas during the 2026 Food & Wine Festival. The fan-favorite experience was missing from recent festival lineups.

parkioInsight.text: Early breakfast reservations at Chefs de France often allow quick access to World Showcase before the 11am rope drop. Worth booking as soon as the festival window opens.

bignews.title: Disney restricts bus and boat access between Disney Springs and resorts
bignews.body: New policies limit free transportation between Disney Springs and WDW hotels, with updated rules for on-site guest resort hopping and parking. Third-party resort guests face stricter access limits.

parkioInsight.text: Guests planning evening Disney Springs visits from resorts may need to budget extra time for ride-share or personal vehicles. Bus frequency tends to drop after 9pm regardless of new policies.

topstories.title: Toy Story 5 merchandise and experiences live across parks
topstories.body: New character designs, themed food items, and collectibles tied to the film's June 19 theatrical release are now available at Walt Disney World and Disneyland. Fresh meet-and-greet opportunities are also rolling out.

parkioInsight.text: Toy Story merchandise at flagship locations like Once Upon a Toy tends to sell out within the first few days of film releases. Morning shopping runs usually offer the best selection.

topstories.title: Indiana Jones Adventure Outpost now hosts caricature artists
topstories.body: The permanently closed merchandise location at Hollywood Studios has been repurposed for character caricature services. The outpost had been shuttered since early 2025.

parkioInsight.text: Caricature wait times are typically shortest during mid-afternoon when most guests are queuing for attractions. Sessions usually run 15–20 minutes per guest.

topstories.title: Star Tours merch refresh at Hollywood Studios
topstories.body: A new Star Tours t-shirt is now available at Tatooine Traders, and the StarSpeeder play set has been discounted by over $150 both in-park and online at the Disney Store.

parkioInsight.text: Tatooine Traders tends to be less crowded in the first park hour and after 7pm. The StarSpeeder discount may signal limited remaining inventory.

topstories.title: Sequined Minnie and Maleficent ear headbands debut
topstories.body: Two new ear designs—one featuring pink, purple, and blue sequins, the other inspired by Maleficent—are now on sale at Animal Kingdom's Riverside Depot for $36.99 each.

parkioInsight.text: Limited-edition ear headbands often sell out at the debut location within a week. Check Riverside Depot early in your Animal Kingdom visit if these are a priority.

topstories.title: Tiana's Bayou Adventure queue honors military service
topstories.body: New photos reveal queue elements highlighting Tiana's father as a decorated World War I veteran, adding historical depth to the attraction's backstory.

parkioInsight.text: The queue's storytelling details are easier to appreciate during low-wait windows, which tend to occur in the final two park hours. Morning waits often exceed 60 minutes.

topstories.title: Port Orleans Resort rolls out new beignets and Boatwright's menu
topstories.body: Disney's Port Orleans Resort has introduced updated beignet flavors and a refreshed menu at Boatwright's Dining Hall, expanding the resort's Cajun-inspired food offerings.

parkioInsight.text: Resort dining at Port Orleans is usually easier to access without a reservation during weekday lunch windows. Beignets are often freshest before 10am.

icymi.title: Lion medallions replace Zootopia designs at Animal Kingdom
icymi.body: The collectible medallion machine at Discovery Trading Company now features four lion designs instead of the Zootopia: Better Zoogether graphics that had been available since summer 2025.

icymi.title: Oogie Boogie Bash Halloween night sold out
icymi.body: Despite slower overall ticket sales compared to Magic Kingdom's Halloween party, the October 31 date for Disneyland's Oogie Boogie Bash has reached capacity for 2026.

icymi.title: Disney Lakeshore Lodge construction updates shared
icymi.body: Disney Parks Blog launched a new series titled Letters from Lakeshore, offering behind-the-scenes progress reports on the nature-inspired resort being built on the south shore of Bay Lake.

spotlight.title: Full Eat to the Beat concert schedule released for Food & Wine
spotlight.body: EPCOT's live entertainment team has published the complete lineup for the 2026 Food & Wine Festival concert series at America Gardens Theatre. The schedule features returning acts and several festival debuts.

parkioInsight.text: America Gardens Theatre seating tends to fill 30–45 minutes before popular headliners. Arriving during the prior set usually secures better sightlines without a long wait.
